mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and contracting consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with an accuracy above 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher than the market baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of cases, invalidating the supply-constraint transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 270 reflects accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate demonstrates robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no directional bias when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new housing starts signal strengthening residential demand and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts data shows zero correlation with 60-day SPX direction, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index surpassing 99.5 indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of threshold-crossing instances, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
